blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Taller than average. The way he sat — back straight even at ease. At the edge of the group rather than the center.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That matches the individual who was found tonight.
Julian set his mug down on the porch rail.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Then the one watching him was someone who knew they were looking at a man who wouldn't be there much longer.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Or they were looking at a friend. People look at their friends, Mr. Crane. Especially in a group, at a fire, at night.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: But the quality of the looking was wrong. You spend enough time watching people watch people and you know the difference between someone who cares about a person and someone who is tracking a person. This was tracking.
The night was very quiet. The ocean was a constant underneath everything.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You understand this is a significant thing to say.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I'm not implying anything. I'm telling you what I observed. What it means is your job.
Davenport looked at him for a long moment. Then he looked at the house next door. Then at the hat in his hands — he'd taken it off again without noticing.
